acceptance_criteria.when: the following Python check runs: import json; data = json.load(open('policies/tool-policy.v1.json')); roles_with_prefix = {r for r, v in data['roles'].items() if '*/docs/codex/' in v.get('allowed_write_path_prefixes', [])}
acceptance_criteria.then: roles_with_prefix equals exactly {'ba', 'qa', 'pm', 'architect', 'product-owner', 'user', 'ui-specialist', 'spec', 'prompt-inspector', 'cleanliness-inspector', 'style-inspector'} (11 roles, no more, no less); AND data['roles']['dev']['allowed_write_path_prefixes'] still contains '*' (dev NOT in roles_with_prefix, wildcard unchanged); AND no other role beyond the 11 listed has gained */docs/codex/

check.script: import json; data = json.load(open('policies/tool-policy.v1.json')); roles_with_prefix = {r for r, v in data['roles'].items() if '*/docs/codex/' in v.get('allowed_write_path_prefixes', [])}; expected = {'ba', 'qa', 'pm', 'architect', 'product-owner', 'user', 'ui-specialist', 'spec', 'prompt-inspector', 'cleanliness-inspector', 'style-inspector'}; assert roles_with_prefix == expected, f'Role set mismatch: got {roles_with_prefix}, expected {expected}'; assert '*' in data['roles']['dev']['allowed_write_path_prefixes'], 'dev wildcard removed'; print('AC1 PASS')
import json; data = json.load(open('policies/tool-policy.v1.json')); assert data['policy_version'] == 4, f'Expected 4, got {data["policy_version"]}'; print('AC2 PASS')

acceptance_criteria.then: it contains a path-safety guard checking CODEX_OUT_TASK_ID and CODEX_OUT_ROLE against ^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$; AND the guard explicitly rejects '.' and '..' as standalone values (Codex CF-2: '..' passes base regex alone but can escape docs/codex/ at shell runtime); AND when both vars pass all guards the canonical path shape is $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex/<task_id>/<role>.txt (two-level hierarchy, not flat); AND mkdir -p is called for the per-task directory; AND when either var is absent or unsafe path falls back to /var/tmp/codex-outputs/codex-output-$$-$(date +%s).txt

acceptance_criteria.when: all physical tee invocations in both files are inspected (regardless of branch count)
acceptance_criteria.then: zero occurrences of 'tee "$CODEX_OUT"' or 'tee "${CODEX_OUT}"' exist in EITHER file; AND every physical tee target in both files is one of: '$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex/$CODEX_OUT_TASK_ID/$CODEX_OUT_ROLE.txt' (canonical branch), or '/var/tmp/codex-outputs/codex-output-$$-$(date +%s).txt' (fallback literal); AND command-substitution tee forms 'tee "$(...)"' or 'tee $(...)' are absent from both files

checks.description: All tee targets in commands/codex.md are hook-resolvable (no unrecognized tee targets)
All tee targets in SKILL.md are hook-resolvable (no unrecognized tee targets)

acceptance_criteria.when: Step 2 path-resolution block is compared to commands/codex.md Step 2
acceptance_criteria.then: both contain the same path-safety guard (including '.' and '..' exclusion) and same branch structure; AND SKILL.md Source: cross-link at line 8 is preserved unchanged

checks.description: SKILL.md contains canonical docs/codex path with task_id

acceptance_criteria.when: Step 2 block is read in each file
acceptance_criteria.then: /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ appears ONLY in the else/fallback branch (not in the canonical if-branch) in BOTH files; AND the primary/default branch produces a docs/codex/<task_id>/<role>.txt path
